stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation,' confirming its primary application in generative AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of text-generation using transformer-based models.
The mention of Llama and Hugging Face indicates relevance within the current large language model ecosystem.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels.
The dataset is designed for visual generative modeling and is fully permissively licensed for research and commercial use.
GPIC includes 100M training examples, is safety-filtered, and deduplicated, addressing major data availability challenges in AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline associated with the model is text-generation, indicating its primary function.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the mainstream LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, offering a faster local AI experience for Apple users.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the critical lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only its generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing of LLM behavior and failure modes by analyzing their underlying 'digital DNA'.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a significant industry bottleneck: PCB design is currently manual and highly expertise-dependent.
The core technical challenge overcome involves developing an LLM-suited representation and addressing the scarcity of large-scale PCB datasets.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal metric, the compositional residual (eps*), to quantify the degree of global incoherence.
Provides a runtime method to compute this incoherence based on system output and coupling constraints.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS),' a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus.
The tool allows for post-hoc auditing of LLM behavior and failure modes using only the model's generated text.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses the current manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB design, a gap in generative AI applications.
The core technical challenge addressed is developing an LLM-suited representation and dataset for this domain.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The work formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus.
It allows researchers to audit or diagnose the composition of an LLM using only the text it generates, which is a significant step for model accountability.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses the critical industry bottleneck of PCB design, which is currently manual and expertise-intensive.
Overcomes key challenges by developing an LLM-suited representation and addressing the lack of large-scale datasets in this domain.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a fundamental failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal metric, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
The metric is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and defined cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation by moving beyond static datasets to clinically realistic EHR settings.
Introduces MedCase-Structured, a new text-to-FHIR dataset designed for benchmarking diagnostic reasoning.
Provides a pipeline to generate structured HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured clinical text, improving model evaluation realism.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents entirely on local, personal hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Microsoft is threatening legal action for disclosing exploits
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is under scrutiny for its response to zero-day exploit disclosures.
A figure named 'Nightmare Eclipse' is publicly sharing proof-of-concept exploit code against Microsoft.
Cybersecurity researcher Kevin Beaumont is analyzing Microsoft's handling of the disclosed exploits.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
The story falls into the category of cybersecurity threat intelligence.

stories.title: Startup offers free home cleaning—if it can record it all for robot training
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: A startup is offering free home cleaning services in exchange for data collection.
The data collected involves humans wearing head cameras to record activities for robot training.
This represents another iteration of using human labor for gathering large-scale training data for AI/robotics.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude models) in scientific software development, specifically building a differentiable perturbation theory module (CLAX-PT) in JAX.
It presents a quantified case study detailing 15 supervision events over 12 work days, classifying the level of human intervention required.
The findings show the AI agent achieved significant autonomy, resolving ten tasks independently through iterative testing, supplemented by physicist domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: VideoMLA introduces Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) to video diffusion models.
It addresses memory and latency issues by replacing per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent KV cache.
The work builds upon existing fixed-size sliding-window KV cache techniques in long-rollout causal video diffusion.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential/time-series data.
Highlights a key research gap: existing benchmarks lack natural-language rationales for anomaly detection, hindering VLM interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to bridge the gap between VLM performance and grounded, interpretable time-series anomaly detection.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLMs where reasoning is tied to external, autoregressive token generation.
It proposes mimicking human cognition by using internal 'working memory' to manipulate information without externalizing intermediate thoughts.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from external communication to improve the LLMs' latent reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: New research proposes an alternative to Reinforcement Learning for enhancing frontier reasoning models.
The method involves sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base model, potentially bypassing costly post-training.
A key practical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately 'mix' into this power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses the limitation of current pipelines that rely on static visual encoders, pushing motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.
The framework constructs image-language-3D flow triplets from heterogeneous data to better capture action-relevant scene aspects.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) in Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a new method designed to make TTFT practical by improving efficiency.
The core innovation involves using Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching to balance the trade-off between speed and the quality of adaptation.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) regarding biased and unstable learning due to fixed aggregation weights.
It introduces the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to improve fairness and stability in collaborative model training.
FL itself is a relevant paradigm for privacy-sensitive, distributed data training across multiple clients.

stories.title: When, why, and how do diffusion posterior samplers fail? A finite-sample lens
stories.author: Sara Fridovich-Keil
bullet_points: The paper investigates the failure modes of diffusion posterior samplers, a technique used in imaging inverse problems.
Diffusion models are noted for their strong capability in modeling complex natural data distributions.
A key issue addressed is the reliance on inexact likelihood approximations during intermediate timesteps, which negatively impacts the final sampled posterior.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: distinguishing good research proposals from poor ones before resource expenditure.
The benchmark is constructed from 1,099 machine learning research proposals sourced from ICLR submissions.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method suggests that reasoning capabilities in large language models can be achieved by sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base model.
This approach potentially bypasses the need for expensive post-training techniques like Reinforcement Learning (RL) or curated datasets.
The main technical hurdle identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw samples from this newly defined power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in RLHF: the reliance on static reward models which struggle with diverse and heterogeneous human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to adapt to unseen preference domains without requiring costly retraining.
This research aims to improve the robustness and generalization capabilities of LLMs when aligning them with complex human values.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, which couples internal computation to external token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition, utilizing internal 'working memory' to manipulate information without externalizing intermediate thoughts.
The goal is to decouple reasoning from the autoregressive generation process to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: Introduces 'COMPOSE,' a method for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The approach addresses the limitations of existing methods by considering both prior work direction and formal structural dependencies.
Aims to create 'grounded future mathematical generation' for a given anchor paper.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for improving frontier reasoning models.
It introduces the concept of a 'power distribution' derived from the base model, which can elicit comparable reasoning abilities without extensive post-training.
The main technical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw samples from this newly defined power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings fail to meet conventional resolution targets.
Specific findings show that 11 of 40 comparisons on the Open LLM Leaderboard v1 and 4 of 9 MMLU-Pro pairs were unresolved under standard design.
The paper proposes framing paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem to improve diagnostic capabilities.

stories.title: OpenRouter raises $113M Series B
stories.author: freeCandy
bullet_points: OpenRouter secured $113M in a Series B funding round.
The news indicates significant investor confidence in the AI infrastructure/API aggregation space.
OpenRouter operates in the competitive field of managing and routing access to various large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: I Made My AI Models Argue, Then Let Hermes Be the Judge
stories.author: arqamwd
bullet_points: The article describes a system where multiple LLMs engage in a debate.
A specific LLM, named Hermes, is used to act as the judge in the debate.
The system aims to create a 'multi-model decision agent' that learns model trustworthiness.

stories.title: Inference Theft Is the New AI App Security Bug: How to Protect Your LLM Endpoints
stories.author: nimay_04
bullet_points: Addresses the emerging security risk of 'Inference Theft' in LLM applications.
Provides a practical checklist for securing public AI endpoints.
Covers specific threats like model abuse, runaway agent loops, and unexpected billing costs.
